key_insight: A physics-informed multihead neural network can learn a low-dimensional latent manifold for PDE solution families, revealing robust principal components and spectral profiles beyond the initial condition.
engineering_relevance: This provides a reusable way to compress, analyze, and compare large families of PDE solutions with stable geometric features that could aid reduced-order modeling and scientific discovery.
method: Shared network body learns a latent embedding of the solution manifold.
Multiple linear heads reconstruct solutions for different initial conditions.
Head-orthogonalization penalizes degeneracies and stabilizes the latent basis; Fourier-shell analysis measures frequency contributions to each principal component.
evidence_signal: On the 1D viscous Burgers equation, a latent dimension of 20 yielded only 2-4 principal components for about 95% variance and 4-7 for about 99%, with similar compression behavior on heat and wave equation checks.
watch_item: Because the initial condition is built into the output by construction, the latent components capture residual variation on top of the initial profile, so interpretation depends on the chosen architecture and basis freedom.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Neural Embeddings of PDE Solution Families
